summary.narrative: On these campaign commitments, Johnson fully delivered several executive-authority items at the start of his term — ending the ShotSpotter contract, creating a Deputy Mayor for Immigrant, Migrant and Refugee Rights, and signing youth-employment and detective-recruitment orders. Larger revenue-dependent and legislative pledges stalled: the Bring Chicago Home referendum was defeated by voters, the corporate head tax was rejected by the City Council, and none of the $800 million in promised new taxes passed. His pledge not to raise property taxes was reported as broken when his FY2025 budget proposed an increase.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows a split pattern: high delivery on items within direct executive control (ShotSpotter, deputy mayor, executive orders) but low delivery on high-clarity, high-magnitude legislative and revenue promises that depended on voters and the City Council, plus a reversed negative promise on property taxes. The strongest-clarity, most ambitious commitments are precisely those that stalled or reversed, while the delivered items skew toward narrower or symbolic magnitude.

promises.text: Johnson pledged throughout the campaign that he would not raise property taxes if elected.
what_happened: 2024-10-30: Johnson unveiled FY2025 budget that included a property tax increase, described as breaking his campaign promise (c88, c95)
2025-05-09: Reporting cites a 'broken promise to hold the line on property taxes' (c90)

data_gaps: Campaign-window dating is sparse (13 of 69 dated); several promise candidates are in-office or date_uncertain and were not treated as attributable promises. Detective and youth-employment 'full' targets lack a confirming source for completion — flagged LIMITED EVIDENCE. Property-tax classification is CONTESTED (city framing vs. third-party watchdog framing). Many corroborating delivery sources are TIER 3 (Illinois Policy Institute appears repeatedly and is an advocacy source); Tier-1 city press releases (c72, c99, c77) corroborate executive actions but tend toward self-claimed accomplishment language — treat with care for CREDIT OVERCLAIMED on broad anniversary claims (c77).

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 77.5% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
